name: Rwanda

colors.symbolism: hope of prosperity, the country's natural resources, and productivity

description: Three horizontal stripes — a wide light-blue band at the top (half the flag), a narrower yellow band, and a narrower green band at the bottom. A yellow sun with twenty-four straight rays sits in the upper fly on the blue band.
history: Adopted on 25 October 2001, replacing the red-yellow-green Pan-African tricolour with a black 'R' (in use since independence in 1962) which had become associated with the 1994 genocide against the Tutsi. The new design — by Alphonse Kirimobenecyo, an artist and engineer — was chosen to symbolise national unity, respect for work, and heroism, distancing the country from its previous flag.

features.symbolism: a deliberate departure from the previous red-yellow-green Pan-African tricolour (which carried associations with the 1994 genocide); the new colours signal happiness, peace, prosperity, and hope for the future
enlightenment; a guide against ignorance and a symbol of unity, transparency, and the fight against ignorance
